assertions.text: Verifies actual assembled context before diagnosing

with_harness.evidence: Log the full assembled prompt sent to the model. Dump the raw API request body

without_harness.evidence: No mention of logging or verifying actual prompt

assertions.text: Identifies MISSING CONTEXT (retrieval may return wrong docs)

with_harness.evidence: retrieval failure masquerading as a generation failure

without_harness.evidence: Many hallucination issues are actually retrieval failures

assertions.text: Identifies BURIED CONTEXT (docs in low-attention position)

with_harness.evidence: docs likely buried in the middle... lost in the middle effect

without_harness.evidence: Attention dilution... lost in the middle

assertions.text: Identifies CONFLICTING CONTEXT (helpfulness vs grounding instruction)

with_harness.evidence: tension between general helpfulness and grounding constraint... emergent conflicts

without_harness.evidence: be helpful and comprehensive alongside only use provided documents

with_harness.evidence: Full table; explicit Compound Failure: BURIED + CONFLICTING + MISSING

assertions.text: Distinguishes retrieval failures from grounding failures

with_harness.evidence: retrieval failure masquerading as generation failure + manual check protocol

without_harness.evidence: Quick diagnostic test separating retrieval from generation quality

with_harness.evidence: Place documents last, explicit delimiters, replace vague grounding instruction with specific operational one

without_harness.evidence: Move documents out of system prompt, require citations, add refusal instruction

behavioral_observations.approach_difference: WITH follows structured Step 0/1/2 triage. WITHOUT uses domain-expert ad-hoc analysis.
behavioral_observations.precision_difference: WITH uses explicit category labels and compound diagnosis. WITHOUT describes same issues in prose.
behavioral_observations.quality_difference: Baseline is very strong on RAG problems — nearly indistinguishable from with-harness quality. Only Step 0 differentiates.

suggestions.reason: Non-discriminating — baseline already handles RAG diagnosis well. Need harder assertions.
Neither version checks whether the system prompt has too many instructions relative to document tokens.

assertion_feedback.overall: Only 1 discriminating assertion out of 7. Baseline is too strong for these assertions. Consider harder RAG-specific scenarios.
